title: wahlenbergia subpilosa
intro: Wahlenbergia subpilosa is a plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family and is commonly known as the New Zealand bluebell. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Wahlenbergia subpilosa include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Wahlenbergia subpilosa typically involve infusions and decoctions made from the plant's leaves and roots, which can be used to relieve symptoms and promote overall well-being.
